subject: 🔄 Claude Live Artifacts: auto-refresh dashboards + Amazon $5B compute
snippet: Auto-refreshing dashboards pull live data from your apps, 5 gigawatts of compute capacity by 2026 Stay updated with today's top AI news, papers, and repos. Signup | Work With Us | Follow on X | Archive Hey Aikkara, Claude is having a big da…
body_html:
Auto-refreshing dashboards pull live data from your apps, 5 gigawatts of compute capacity by 2026
Stay updated with today's top AI news, papers, and repos.
Signup | Work With Us | Follow on X | Archive

Hey Aikkara,
Claude is having a big day. Live Artifacts ships for real-time dashboards, and Amazon drops $5B to fix the infrastructure strain that came with Claude's explosive growth.

The pattern is clear: Claude is no longer a chatbot, it's becoming the operating layer for work. That demands serious compute. 5 gigawatts serious.

OpenAI mocked Anthropic for not having enough compute. That aged poorly.
Today's Author
Lior Alexander. Founder of AlphaSignal and former ML Engineer. Previously built ML systems at Iguazio, Guesty, Enphase, Mila.
Summary

Read time: 5 min 51 sec

Top News

▸ Claude now builds live dashboards that auto-refresh with your real data

Top News

▸ Amazon doubles down on Anthropic with $5B now and 5 gigawatts of compute

Lambda

▸ How to push Model FLOPS Utilization past 60%

Top Repo

▸ Open-source tool uses Claude to find jobs, fill forms, and rewrite your CV

Signals

1. Open-source desktop tool turns any image into a 3D model on your GPU
2. Open-source AI hedge fund simulates 13 legendary investors making trades
3. New framework redefines software engineering in the age of AI agents
4. Intel shrinks Qwen3.6-35B to int4, making it run on a single GPU
5. Claude Code's 'Find Skills' plugin auto-recommends the right tools for any task
Top News
Claude now builds live dashboards that auto-refresh with your real data
10,683 Likes

Claude just shipped Live Artifacts inside Cowork, and it changes how you build internal tools.

Here is the idea. You ask Claude to build a dashboard or tracker. It connects that thing directly to your apps and files. Every time you open it, it pulls fresh data automatically. No manual refresh. No stale numbers.

What makes this actually useful:

  • Auto-refreshing dashboards that stay current without you touching them
  • Connected to your real files and apps, not dummy data
  • Saved in a Live Artifacts tab with full version history, so you can roll back anytime
  • Accessible across any session, pick up exactly where you left off

This means you can build a project tracker, a metrics dashboard, or a data monitor once, and it just works going forward. No rebuilding. No copy-pasting numbers.

It is available now on all paid plans. Update the Claude app and look for it inside Cowork.
READ MORE
forward →
Top News
Amazon doubles down on Anthropic with $5B now and 5 gigawatts of compute
9,190 Likes

Claude has been struggling. A sharp rise in consumer usage has led to strain on Anthropic's infrastructure, impacting reliability and performance. So Anthropic just made a massive move to fix that.

A new agreement with Amazon secures up to 5 gigawatts of capacity for training and deploying Claude, with nearly 1GW of Trainium2 and Trainium3 capacity coming online by end of 2026. Think of gigawatts here as raw computing muscle. More of it means faster responses, fewer outages, and bigger models.

Here is what this means practically:

  • You can now access the full Claude Platform directly inside your AWS account, with no extra credentials or contracts.
  • Expanded inference capacity is coming to Asia and Europe, so latency drops globally.
  • Anthropic's run-rate revenue has surpassed $30 billion, up from $9 billion at end of 2025, meaning Claude is clearly being used heavily in production.

Amazon is investing $5 billion now, with up to $20 billion more tied to commercial milestones. More compute, more stability, better Claude for you.
READ MORE
forward →
Presented by Lambda
How to push Model FLOPS Utilization past 60%

Most large-scale training runs operate at 35-45% MFU. You're paying for twice the compute you're using.

Lambda's engineers benchmarked Llama 3.1 (8B-405B) on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs and traced losses to:

  • Memory overhead
  • Parallelism strategy
  • Serialized communication

The result: a reproducible framework that pushed MFU past 60% with no model changes.
GET THE GUIDE
partner with us →
Top Repo
Open-source tool uses Claude to find jobs, fill forms, and rewrite your CV
4,075 Likes

Someone got tired of the job search grind and built career-ops, an open-source AI tool that automates the entire job hunt. The creator used it personally, evaluated 740+ offers, applied to 66, got 12 interviews, and landed a Head of Applied AI role. Then open-sourced it.

Here is what it actually does for you:

  • Scans job portals automatically, including Greenhouse, Ashby, Lever, and company pages
  • Evaluates fit by reasoning about your CV vs the job description, not just keyword matching
  • Generates tailored, ATS-optimized CVs customized per job description and processes 10+ offers in parallel
  • Flags suspicious ghost job listings using an independent legitimacy check

To get started: clone the repo, configure your resume and preferences, then launch modes like auto-pipeline, scan, batch, or pdf. Your CV and personal data stay on your machine.

Key thing: it does not auto-submit. It automates analysis, not decisions. You read every report before applying. It has 36K+ GitHub stars already.
READ MORE
forward →
